results.comment_text:
@user1:
Why these specific whitespace characters and not just  \s?

@author:
At first, I used `\s`, but was receiving this deprecation warning:

```
scripts/validate_docstrings.py:695
  /Users/danielcosta/pandas-daniel/scripts/validate_docstrings.py:695: DeprecationWarning: invalid escape sequence \s
    '\n\s*return' +
```

[re documentation](https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html) shows that `\s` is equivalente to `[ \t\n\r\f\v]`, so I just removed the end line and carriage return (`\n` and `\r`), which are not what I intend to consume there, and left the others. Sincerely, I couldn't find out the meaning of `\f` and `\v`.

That said, I just tested using `\s` again and it seems that while using `re.VERBOSE` I don't get any more warnings. That leaves me wondering if using `\s` in regex is really discouraged by some reason or if it was just a wrongful warning that I was receiving. Maybe you can help me clarifying this.

I'll hold on with making the substitution until then.

@author:
I found out that the deprecation warning didn't go away with the `re.VERBOSE`, but with the usage of a raw string, and now I understand why it was happening in the first place.

Thus I replaced `[ \t\f\v]` for `\s`.
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
def test_validate_docstrings_uses_ast_based_return_detection_not_regex__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/scripts/validate_docstrings.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'def method_returns_something' in source, 'Expected an AST-based helper `method_returns_something` to exist in scripts/validate_docstrings.py (added in the reviewed change).'

def test_validate_docstrings_uses_ast_based_return_detection_not_regex__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/scripts/validate_docstrings.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'if doc.method_returns_something:' in source, 'Expected get_validation_data to use `doc.method_returns_something` to decide whether RT01 should be raised when Returns section is missing.'

def test_validate_docstrings_uses_ast_based_return_detection_not_regex__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/scripts/validate_docstrings.py', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 're.search(r""" \\"\\"\\".*\\"\\"\\".*  # Skip the docstring.' not in source, 'Found the old regex-based return detection still present. The reviewed change should remove this regex in favor of AST-based detection.'

"""
Analyze docstrings to detect errors.

If no argument is provided, it does a quick check of docstrings and returns
a csv with all API functions and results of basic checks.

If a function or method is provided in the form "pandas.function",
"pandas.module.class.method", etc. a list of all errors in the docstring for
the specified function or method.

Usage::
    $ ./validate_docstrings.py
    $ ./validate_docstrings.py pandas.DataFrame.head
"""
import os
import sys
import json
import re
import glob
import functools
import collections
import argparse
import pydoc
import inspect
import importlib
import doctest
import tempfile

import flake8.main.application

try:
    from io import StringIO
except ImportError:
    from cStringIO import StringIO

# Template backend makes matplotlib to not plot anything. This is useful
# to avoid that plot windows are open from the doctests while running the
# script. Setting here before matplotlib is loaded.
# We don't warn for the number of open plots, as none is actually being opened
os.environ['MPLBACKEND'] = 'Template'
import matplotlib
matplotlib.rc('figure', max_open_wa...'
                           'docstring names, in order to decide which ones '
                           'will be validated. A prefix "pandas.Series.str.'
                           'will make the script validate all the docstrings'
                           'of methods starting by this pattern. It is '
                           'ignored if parameter function is provided')
    argparser.add_argument('--errors', default=None, help='comma separated '
                           'list of error codes to validate. By default it '
                           'validates all errors (ignored when validating '
                           'a single docstring)')
    argparser.add_argument('--ignore_deprecated', default=False,
                           action='store_true', help='if this flag is set, '
                           'deprecated objects are ignored when validating '
                           'all docstrings')

    args = argparser.parse_args()
    sys.exit(main(args.function, args.prefix,
                  args.errors.split(',') if args.errors else None,
                  args.format,
                  args.ignore_deprecated))
